source: Glacial Lake Expansion Monitoring Using Multi- Temporal SAR and Optical Data Tracking rapid glacial lake growth through advanced satellite remote.pdf
text:
I n t r o d u c t i o n
 Glacial lakesare waterbodiesformed by the melting of glaciers ,  
usually located at glacier margins or in depressions left by retreating
ice.
Due to global warming , glaciers in the Himalayan region are retreating  
rapidly, increasing the number and size of glacial lakes.
Rapid expansion of glacial lakes increases the risk of Glacial Lake  
Outburst Floods (GLOFs) , which occur when natural moraine dams  
fail suddenly.
GLOFs can cause severe flooding, destruction of infrastructure,
agricultural damage, and loss of life .
Therefore, continuous monitoring of glacial lakes  is essential for  
climate change studies and disaster management.
Remote sensing provides an efficient method to observe large and  
inaccessible mountainous areas .

text:
Results
 LakeDetection and Temporal Changes
Key Findings:
NDWI analysis successfully 
extracted lake boundaries from 
Sentinel-2 optical imagery.
Sentinel-1 SAR backscatter 
helped detect water surfaces 
even under cloud cover 
conditions.
Multi-temporal analysis 
revealed changes in lake area 
across different time periods.
Obser vations:
Clear water3land separation 
was achieved using NDWI 
thresholding.
SAR data improved water 
detection reliability in low 
visibility conditions.
Detected expansion and 
shrinkage patterns in lake 
boundaries over time.
Temporal Lake Change Analysis:
Lake boundaries were digitized 
and converted into vector 
layers.
Area statistics were calculated 
for different time periods.
Spatial comparison showed 
significant variations in water 
extent.

text:
Discussion
1
3 4
2
Study Overview
 
Major Findings
Key Achievements
 
Practical Significance
 
Thisstudy demonstrates that remote sensing and
geospatial techniques are highly effective for
monitoring glacial lakes in the Himalayan region.
The combination of optical and radar satellite data 
enables accurate detection of lake boundaries and 
surface changes.
Multi-temporal satellite analysis allows researchers to 
track glacial lake evolution over several years.
Several glacial lakes in the study region increased in
surface area between 2016 and 2024.
Multi-temporal change detection revealed significant 
fluctuations in lake size across different years due 
to rising temperature
Some lakes showed rapid growth near glacier
termini, suggesting active meltwater accumulation.
The results highlight the growing influence of climate 
change on Himalayan cryospheric systems.
Thestudy helpsidentifyglacial lakes with potential 
Glacial Lake Outburst Flood (GLOF) risks.
Provides valuable geospatia

source: Glacial Lake Expansion Monitoring Using Multi- Temporal SAR and Optical Data Tracking rapid glacial lake growth through advanced satellite remote.pdf
text:
malayan cryospheric systems.
Thestudy helpsidentifyglacial lakes with potential 
Glacial Lake Outburst Flood (GLOF) risks.
Provides valuable geospatial data for disaster 
management authorities.
Supports long-term climate monitoring and 
environmental research.
Enables policymakers to develop early warning 
systems and mitigation strategies for vulnerable 
downstream communities.
Demonstrates the usefulness of remote sensing 
technologies for sustainable mountain ecosystem 
management.
Successful integrationof Sentinel-1 SAR data and 
Sentinel-2 optical imagery for comprehensive lake
monitoring.
Application of NDWI (Normalized Difference Water 
Index) enabled clear extraction of water bodies from 
surrounding land and ice.
Development of a systematic workflow using Google 
Earth Engine and QGIS for efficient data processing 
and mapping.
Accurate vectorization of lake boundaries for further 
spatial and temporal analysis.
